plan_metrics.plan_content:
## Plan: Add `search` Command

**Two files to touch — nothing else needed.**

### 1. Create `src/search.py` (new file)

Four functions using only stdlib (`urllib.parse.urlparse`):

| Function | Logic |
|---|---|
| `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` | `query.lower() in b.title.lower()` — case-insensitive substring |
| `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` | `tag in b.tags` — exact list membership |
| `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` | `urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain` — exact netloc equality |
| `search(bookmarks, title, tag, domain)` | Chains the above; each non-`None` criterion narrows the set (AND logic); no criteria → returns all |

**Key nuance on domain:** The test `test_matches_subdomain` passes `"docs.python.org"` as the full domain string and expects exact `netloc` equality — `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/...").netloc` returns `"docs.python.org"`, so exact equality is the right implementation.

### 2. Modify `src/cli.py`

- **Import:** `from .search import search`
- **Parser:** Add `search` subparser with `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain` (all `default=None`) and `--format` (`choices=["table","json"]`, `default="table"`)
- **Handler:** Load bookmarks → call `search()` → print via existing `format_table` / `format_json`

**No new formatter code** — reuses existing `format_table` and `format_json` from `src/formatter.py` exactly as the `list` command does.

### Verification
```bash
pytest tests/ -v   # all tests in test_storage.py + test_search.py should pass
```
